agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A digital wallet platform enabling peer-to-peer and entity money transfers with tiered identity verification, regulatory compliance (KYC/AML), and account security controls. The product operates in a regulated financial services space where compliance failures carry legal and financial penalties.",
    "bounded_context": "Wallet Transactions & Account Security",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "KYC (Know Your Customer)", "definition": "Identity verification process that determines an account's verification tier and unlocks sending/withdrawal capabilities." },
      { "term": "Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed full KYC. Permitted to send, receive, and withdraw funds up to the daily transfer limit of $5,000." },
      { "term": "Partially-Verified Account", "definition": "An account that has completed some but not all KYC steps. Can send and receive funds with a reduced daily transfer limit of $500." },
      { "term": "Unverified Account", "definition": "An account with no KYC completion. May receive funds only — cannot send or withdraw." },
      { "term": "Settlement", "definition": "The finalization of a transaction, occurring at T+1 (one business day after initiation), after which the transaction becomes irreversible." },
      { "term": "Sanctioned Entity", "definition": "A person, organization, or country appearing on a regulatory sanctions list (e.g., OFAC). All transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked." },
      { "term": "Daily Transfer Limit", "definition": "The maximum aggregate outbound transfer amount a user may initiate within a rolling 24-hour or calendar-day window." },
      { "term": "Account Lock", "definition": "A security state triggered by 5 consecutive failed login attempts. The account is frozen and can only be unlocked by a support agent — not by the user." },
      { "term": "Transaction Notification", "definition": "An alert sent to the account holder for any single transaction exceeding $1,000 in value." },
      { "term": "T+1", "definition": "Settlement occurs one business day after the transaction is initiated. Prior to settlement, the transaction is pending; after settlement, it is final and irreversible." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "An account must complete full KYC verification before it is permitted to send money or withdraw funds.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Allowing unverified users to send money violates financial regulations (AML/KYC). This is a legal and data-integrity constraint — the system is in a corrupt/non-compliant state if violated.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to initiate a send or withdrawal from an account with verification_status = 'unverified' MUST be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Unverified accounts may receive funds but may not send or withdraw funds.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is the regulatory floor — receiving is permissible to avoid blocking legitimate inbound payments, but outbound movement without identity is a compliance violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "An inbound transfer to an unverified account MUST succeed. An outbound transfer or withdrawal from an unverified account MUST be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $5,000. Partially-verified accounts have a daily outbound transfer limit of $500.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Dollar thresholds are business policy decisions that could be adjusted by product leadership or regulatory guidance without indicating system corruption.",
      "testable_assertion": "A verified account attempting to send an amount that would cause total daily outbound transfers to exceed $5,000 MUST be rejected. Same for partially-verified at $500."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "A transaction cannot be reversed once it has reached settled status (T+1 after initiation).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Reversal of a settled transaction would corrupt financial ledger integrity and violate the settlement finality principle. If this is violated, the system's accounting state is inconsistent.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any reversal request for a transaction with status = 'settled' MUST be rejected. Only transactions with status = 'pending' may be reversed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "All transfers to sanctioned entities must be automatically blocked before execution.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Transferring funds to a sanctioned entity is a violation of law (e.g., OFAC regulations). Allowing such a transfer means the system has entered an illegal state — this is non-negotiable.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any transfer where the recipient matches a sanctioned entity list MUST be blocked and MUST NOT move funds. The transaction must be flagged for compliance review."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "After 5 consecutive failed login attempts, the account must be locked. Only a support agent can unlock it.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is an account security integrity constraint. If a locked account can be unlocked by the user themselves or by automated means, the brute-force protection is defeated and the security state is compromised.",
      "testable_assertion": "After 5 consecutive failed login attempts, subsequent login attempts (even with correct credentials) MUST be rejected until a support agent explicitly unlocks the account."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "A notification must be sent for any single transaction exceeding $1,000.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The $1,000 notification threshold and the notification mechanism itself are business policy preferences. A missed notification is a UX failure, not a data corruption or compliance violation. The threshold could be changed by product management.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a transaction with amount > $1,000 is processed, a notification MUST be dispatched to the account holder. Transactions of $1,000 or less do NOT trigger this notification."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Partially-verified accounts are permitted to send funds (within their $500 daily limit) but must complete full KYC to access the $5,000 tier.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The existence and privileges of a 'partially-verified' tier is a product/policy decision, not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "A partially-verified account sending $400 MUST succeed. A partially-verified account sending $501 (or exceeding $500 cumulative daily) MUST be rejected."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "An unverified account with a $200 balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $50 to another account",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "An unverified account",
      "when": "Another user sends $500 to this unverified account",
      "then": "The transfer succeeds and the unverified account balance increases by $500"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A verified account that has already sent $4,800 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $250",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it would exceed the $5,000 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A verified account that has sent $0 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $5,000",
      "then": "The transaction succeeds (assuming all other checks pass)"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A partially-verified account that has sent $0 today",
      "when": "The user attempts to send $501",
      "then": "The transaction is rejected because it exceeds the $500 daily limit"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A transaction initiated yesterday that has reached settled status (T+1)",
      "when": "The sender requests a reversal of this transaction",
      "then": "The reversal is rejected with an explanation that settled transactions are final"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A transaction initiated minutes ago that is still in pending status",
      "when": "The sender requests a reversal",
      "then": "The reversal is permitted and funds are returned to the sender"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A verified account attempting to send $2,000",
      "when": "The recipient matches an entry on the sanctions list",
      "then": "The transfer is blocked, no funds are moved, and the transaction is flagged for compliance review"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A user who has failed login 4 times consecutively",
      "when": "The user fails login a 5th time",
      "then": "The account is locked and all subsequent login attempts are rejected"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "The user enters the correct password",
      "then": "Login is still rejected because the account is locked — only a support agent can unlock it"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A locked account",
      "when": "A support agent issues an unlock command",
      "then": "The account is unlocked and the failed login counter is reset to zero"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A verified account sending $1,500",
      "when": "The transaction is successfully processed",
      "then": "A notification is sent to the account holder about this transaction"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A verified account sending $999",
      "when": "The transaction is successfully processed",
      "then": "No high-value transaction notification is sent"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-14",
      "given": "A user who has failed login 4 times, then succeeds on the 5th attempt",
      "when": "The user subsequently fails login once more",
      "then": "The account is NOT locked because the consecutive failure count was reset by the successful login"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-15",
      "given": "An unverified account with a $1,000 balance",
      "when": "The user attempts to withdraw $100",
      "then": "The withdrawal is rejected with an error indicating KYC verification is required"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A transfer of exactly $5,000 by a verified account with $0 sent today", "expected_behavior": "The transfer succeeds — the limit is $5,000 inclusive." },
    { "scenario": "A transfer of exactly $1,000", "expected_behavior": "The spec says 'over $1,000' triggers notification, so exactly $1,000 does NOT trigger a notification. The boundary is strictly greater-than." },
    { "scenario": "A partially-verified account receives funds pushing its balance above $500, then tries to send $500", "expected_behavior": "The send is allowed — the daily limit applies to outbound transfers, not balance. The $500 limit is about how much you send per day, not how much you hold." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple rapid transfers that individually are under the daily limit but collectively exceed it", "expected_behavior": "Each transfer must check cumulative daily outbound total. The system must enforce the limit on aggregate, not per-transaction alone." },
    { "scenario": "A sanctioned entity sends money TO a user's wallet", "expected_behavior": "Spec only states transfers TO sanctioned entities must be blocked. Inbound from sanctioned entities is ambiguous — see escalations. In practice, both directions should be blocked for compliance, but the spec is silent on inbound." },
    { "scenario": "User fails login 5 times, account locks, support unlocks, user immediately fails 5 more times", "expected_behavior": "Account locks again. The rule re-applies from a clean state after unlock." },
    { "scenario": "Transaction initiated on a Friday — does T+1 mean Saturday or Monday?", "expected_behavior": "T+1 in financial contexts typically means the next business day (Monday), but the spec does not clarify. See escalations." },
    { "scenario": "A $1,200 transfer to a sanctioned entity", "expected_behavior": "The sanctions block takes priority — transaction is blocked. The $1,000 notification should NOT fire because the transaction never completed." },
    { "scenario": "Daily limit window definition: rolling 24 hours vs. calendar day midnight reset", "expected_behavior": "Ambiguous in spec — the definition of 'daily' (rolling vs. calendar) must be clarified as it materially changes enforcement behavior." },
    { "scenario": "Partially-verified user attempts to withdraw funds", "expected_behavior": "Spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw. Partially-verified is not unverified — by implication, partially-verified can withdraw within their $500 daily limit, but this is not explicitly stated." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Incorrect daily limit enforcement or sanctions screening failures expose the company to regulatory fines (potentially millions in OFAC violations). Reversal of settled transactions would create ledger discrepancies and direct financial losses.",
    "operational": "Account locking creates support ticket volume — the support team must be staffed to handle unlock requests. Sanctions list must be kept current (daily or real-time updates). Settlement at T+1 requires a reliable batch or event-driven process.",
    "user": "Unverified users may be frustrated by inability to send/withdraw — clear onboarding UX for KYC is critical. Account locking after 5 attempts protects users but may also lock out legitimate users who forget passwords. The $1,000 notification provides transparency and fraud awareness.",
    "risk": "Highest risk: sanctions screening failure (legal/criminal liability). Second: allowing unverified sends (regulatory non-compliance). Third: settled transaction reversal (financial integrity). Account lockout bypass is a security risk enabling unauthorized access.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "End Users (Wallet Holders)", "impact": "Subject to KYC gates, transfer limits, account locks, and notifications. Experience directly shaped by these rules." },
      { "group": "Compliance / Legal Team", "impact": "Owns sanctions list accuracy, KYC tier definitions, and regulatory reporting. Must be consulted on edge cases around sanctions screening direction and T+1 business day definition." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Sole authority to unlock accounts. Must have tooling and procedures for identity verification before unlocking. Volume directly proportional to lockout frequency." },
      { "group": "Finance / Treasury", "impact": "Settlement finality affects reconciliation processes. Daily limits affect liquidity planning and float management." },
      { "group": "Product Management", "impact": "Owns the policy levers: dollar thresholds, notification triggers, verification tiers. Must balance conversion/UX against compliance rigor." },
      { "group": "Fraud / Risk Team", "impact": "Account locking, transaction notifications, and sanctions screening are primary fraud defense layers. Gaps here directly increase fraud exposure." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 55,
    "reasoning": "The spec is clear on the major rules but leaves several material ambiguities unresolved: (1) whether 'daily' means calendar day or rolling 24 hours, (2) whether T+1 means business days or calendar days, (3) whether inbound transfers from sanctioned entities are also blocked, (4) whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw, and (5) what exactly constitutes 'partially-verified' vs 'unverified'. These ambiguities affect implementation in ways that carry compliance and financial risk.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Unverified accounts cannot send or withdraw",
      "Settled transactions are irreversible",
      "Transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked",
      "5 consecutive failed logins lock the account, only support can unlock",
      "Notifications for transactions over $1,000"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Definition of 'daily' for transfer limits (rolling 24h vs calendar day)",
      "T+1 settlement: business days vs calendar days",
      "Whether inbound transfers FROM sanctioned entities are also blocked",
      "Whether partially-verified accounts can withdraw (spec only says unverified cannot)",
      "What KYC steps distinguish partially-verified from unverified"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Partially-verified accounts CAN send (up to $500/day) — inferred from the existence of a $500 limit for that tier",
      "The $1,000 notification threshold is strictly greater-than (not greater-than-or-equal)",
      "Consecutive failed login count resets to zero on successful login",
      "Sanctions screening occurs before funds are moved, not after",
      "Support agent unlock resets the failed login counter"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The spec defines 'daily transfer limits' but does not specify whether 'daily' means a rolling 24-hour window or a calendar-day reset (e.g., midnight UTC). This materially changes enforcement — a user could send $5,000 at 11:59 PM and another $5,000 at 12:01 AM under a calendar-day model.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Compliance / Legal Team", "End Users"],
      "options": ["Rolling 24-hour window from first transfer", "Calendar day reset at midnight in user's local timezone", "Calendar day reset at midnight UTC"],
      "recommendation": "Calendar day reset at midnight UTC for simplicity and auditability, but this must be confirmed by Product and Compliance."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "T+1 settlement — does this mean one calendar day or one business day? Financial industry standard is business days, but the spec does not state this explicitly.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Treasury", "Compliance / Legal Team", "End Users"],
      "options": ["T+1 calendar days (including weekends/holidays)", "T+1 business days (excluding weekends and bank holidays)"],
      "recommendation": "T+1 business days, consistent with financial industry convention, but must be confirmed."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Sanctions screening direction: the spec says 'transfers to sanctioned entities must be blocked' but is silent on inbound transfers FROM sanctioned entities. OFAC regulations typically require blocking in both directions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Compliance / Legal Team", "End Users", "Fraud / Risk Team"],
      "options": ["Block outbound only (as spec literally states)", "Block both inbound and outbound (as OFAC requires)"],
      "recommendation": "Block both directions. The spec likely omitted inbound by oversight. Legal/Compliance must confirm, as failure to block inbound from sanctioned entities is still a regulatory violation."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Can partially-verified accounts withdraw funds? The spec says unverified accounts cannot withdraw, and partially-verified accounts have a $500 daily transfer limit, but does not explicitly state whether 'transfer limit' includes or excludes withdrawals.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "End Users", "Compliance / Legal Team"],
      "options": ["Partially-verified can withdraw within the $500 daily limit", "Partially-verified cannot withdraw — only fully verified can", "Withdrawals have a separate limit from transfers"],
      "recommendation": "Clarify with Product Management. The conservative approach is to allow withdrawals only for fully verified accounts, but this may harm user experience."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No definition of what KYC steps constitute 'partially-verified' versus 'unverified' versus 'fully verified'. Without this, it is impossible to validate tier transitions or test boundary conditions between tiers.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Compliance / Legal Team", "Engineering"],
      "options": ["Define explicit KYC steps per tier (e.g., email-only = unverified, phone + ID = partial, full document review = verified)"],
      "recommendation": "Product and Compliance must define the verification tier criteria before implementation."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
